notes: The Department of European Languages and Transcultural Studies bring together the former departments of French and Francophone Studies, Germanic Languages, Italian, and Scandinavian. Th term "transcultural" in our name emphasizes our shared European roots, and our expanded focus on the perspectives of filmmakers, writers, and theorists from Africa, Asia, the Caribbean, Central and South America, and elsewhere, allows for a more pointed, rigorous, and comprehensive understand of history and more accurate contextualization of the "European" experience in the world. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer: Two years of college level courses in the declared language or at least one year of high school in which the primary language of instruction was the declared language One course in European literature, culture, film or media. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. Note : High school foreign language classes will NOT satisfy major preparation requirements For information regarding preparation for this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://elts.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
